pattern: Every leading run of ones is already fixed forever, and the two moves let you fold ALL the remaining zeros together and slide the single resulting zero as far right as possible, so the whole answer collapses to a closed-form rearrangement instead of a simulation.
hints: A '1' that already sits before the very first '0' can never be disturbed by either move, so that prefix of the answer is just copied straight from the input.

Rule 1 lets any two touching zeros merge into a single zero further along, and rule 2 lets a lone zero slide rightward past ones one step at a time -- combined, every zero after the first one can eventually be converted away except one.

Count the zeros (z) and ones (o) and the index of the first zero (f); the maximum string is the untouched prefix of f ones, then (z-1) more ones, then a single zero, then the remaining o-f ones -- no simulation needed.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, several scattered zeros collapse into one
statement example, already maximal and untouched
single zero character, no pair or preceding one exists
all zeros, no ones to anchor a prefix
alternating zeros and ones, none adjacent so op1 alone can't touch them directly
zeros clustered at the very front with a single trailing one
zeros clustered at the very end behind a single leading one

stress.description: large random binary string up to the max length
